signals.label: headline finding
signals.deltaText: 10 exact (lat,lng) duplicate groups on 19,612 public stations — each row in §1 is one coordinate pair your registry team can grep today.

signals.label: headline finding
signals.deltaText: 288 cells exceed designed capacity — §1 lists H3 indices + map centers for your ops queue.

signals.label: headline finding
signals.deltaText: 49.13% of UI-reported HONEY sits in the top 20 visible SPL accounts (Solana RPC cap) — economic *shape* for treasury/MM review, not a contributor GPS read.
signals.publicSource: Solana mainnet HONEY SPL mint (public RPC)

signals.label: headline finding
signals.deltaText: KM mapped frozen 33 days at 254,808,022 (no change since 2026-05-01) while registered drivers kept moving (+61 over the same window) — the cumulative coverage counter has stalled even as registrations advance.
signals.publicSource: Natix Coverage Map metrics feed (coverage.natix.network/coverage/v1/metrics) + Solana NATIX SPL mint

questions: On the public cells view, do over-capacity H3 counts match your internal registry and rewards model?
For stations sharing an exact coordinate pair on the public registry, is that expected registration behavior or a dedupe gap worth reconciling?
Is the public Coverage Map metrics feed expected to show flat KM mapped / zero detections while driver registrations keep rising?
Do the largest visible SPL accounts map to known treasury or market-maker custody labels on your side?
threadSeed: Public read on WeatherXM: 288 cells exceed designed capacity — §1 lists H3 indices + map centers for your ops queue. — does that match your internal view, or is the public feed expected to behave this way?
